###
### On the home front: Local Party Politics and Statement on Spoiler Candidates
We heard a lot about Republican [spoiler candidates](https://www.rebekahjonescampaign.com/so/f5NvqHdIM/c?w=ZKe5WHM1wO2xj5U78HLH_eDuDBnn7LMykfM5WdJ06Vg.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY25uLmNvbS8yMDIxLzAzLzE4L3BvbGl0aWNzL2ZyYW5rLWFydGlsZXMtYXJyZXN0ZWQtc2hhbS1jYW5kaWRhdGUtaW52cy9pbmRleC5odG1sIiwiciI6IjA2ZGQ4YTUzLTk0YWEtNDBjYS0wZDFmLTRkMzIzNGJhMjIyYyIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjdlN2E4N2UxLTcwNDMtNDE3OS1iODM4LTVjYTg5MjBmYWFkNyJ9) in Florida during the last election cycle - one in south Florida even [went to jail](https://www.rebekahjonescampaign.com/so/f5NvqHdIM/c?w=eBFL6hfyB0jW-8zWIVFv8hpD3gBGGabudODyUwKtStE.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbnBvc3QuY29tL25hdGlvbi8yMDIxLzAzLzE5L2Zsb3JpZGEtZnJhdWQtYXJ0aWxlcy1yb2RyaWd1ZXotZWxlY3Rpb24vIiwiciI6IjA2ZGQ4YTUzLTk0YWEtNDBjYS0wZDFmLTRkMzIzNGJhMjIyYyIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjdlN2E4N2UxLTcwNDMtNDE3OS1iODM4LTVjYTg5MjBmYWFkNyJ9) [over the scheme](https://www.rebekahjonescampaign.com/so/f5NvqHdIM/c?w=FMx9CVYasYBDSJJr--lD41jY9Fc2WYDZj_tqQ6glAwE.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY25uLmNvbS8yMDIwLzExLzIzL3BvbGl0aWNzL2Zsb3JpZGEtZGFyay1tb25leS1teXN0ZXJ5LWludnMvaW5kZXguaHRtbCIsInIiOiIwNmRkOGE1My05NGFhLTQwY2EtMGQxZi00ZDMyMzRiYTIyMmMiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiI3ZTdhODdlMS03MDQzLTQxNzktYjgzOC01Y2E4OTIwZmFhZDcifQ). Spoiler candidates with similar sounding names or identical last names who don't campaign are the most obvious example of "spoiler candidate recruiting," a practice that I was pleasantly surprised to find out is illegal. But what is it called when a small group of people pay a candidate who doesn't live in the district, cannot win a primary, isn't from the state, can't or doesn't fundraise, and serves only as a name on the ballot to keep away endorsements and financing from larger groups? Is that not also a spoiler candidate scheme, even if the two candidates' names aren't the same? Aside from the ethical issues present when those involved in this scheme hold official titles or positions within the party they're sabotaging (that should disqualify them immediately, right?), what are the legal consequences of engaging in this activity, if any? That's a question my attorneys and I have been trying to figure out these last two months. I've been put through a crash course on local corruption in party politics this past year, and it's shocking to me how small minded people with an ounce power will intentionally sabotage their own community, country, party, even their own ideals, out of pettiness - and it absolutely happens within both political parties. Refuse to kiss the ring of a self-important wealthy socialite? Expect a spoiler candidate to be on the ballot to keep the national party from giving you money, or to keep you from getting major endorsements where the by-laws prohibit them from endorsing before a primary. Become a cross-over candidate with appeal in parties and with NPAs, while becoming the first candidate in 30 years polling within 6 points of the GOP incumbent, with a national platform and international praise for your work and advocacy? Be ready for a small group of people who can't win elections themselves to recruit someone to run against you. If they know they can't beat you, they'll just lie, cheat and sabotage every other candidate on the ballot to punish you for the crime of not needing their support, not wanting the strings that come with their support, and refusing their ultimatums. For me, this came from a very small and isolated group of people that have never won their own races for public office, and found someone who doesn't even live in the district anymore, who moved to the state for the first time in 2020, and admittedly has no chance of winning, to list their name on the ballot for the sole purpose of preventing me from getting full funding and party support, as well as major endorsements. Primary elections in Florida are August 23, 2022, giving candidates a measly 11 weeks to bring in major groups and organizers for fundraising (unless you take dark money before then, which I have not done and will not do). I believe living in the district you want to represent should be a requirement, but it's not. I believe campaign financing should be transparent, but it's not. The second item I plan to fix in Congress by supporting legislation that will overturn Citizens United. Because if you don't live here, work here, send your kids to school here, you can't fully understand the challenges we face as families here every single day -- even absent of a pandemic. We need to normalize electing people who care more about our communities than they do about political favoritism, grabbing power, or grifting off the bloated political infrastructure in this country. I'm the only candidate here who has roots in Florida \- my grandparents are from (and still live in) Florida; my dad even went to Boca High; my son grew up attending Florida's public schools (and still does); and my daughter was born right here in the Panhandle. I went to college in Florida, worked for the state of Florida, and my husband still works for the state of Florida as a scientist - going on six years now. I'm the only one who has fought for Floridians, and risked everything in doing so - including my job, safety and freedom. I'm the only candidate who has polled within striking distance of the GOP incumbent here since 1992, and at the last poll in October, I was only trailing by 6 points. Even the Cook Political Index updated the first district to reflect recent changes we've been making on the ground, slicing off 10 points from its R+ rating. I'm the only candidate who didn't grow up with money, who works with our neighbors to get resources and services they need, and is hitting the pavement every single day to win this election. I'm the only candidate actively recruiting honest, hard-working community leaders to fight for every office in my community so that no corrupt school board member, county commissioner or state legislature runs unopposed. And I do all of this while taking care of my family, managing my data systems, filming a documentary, suing the state, and continuing my work as a science advocate in the media. If you're wondering why on Earth any Democrat would be engaged in activities that could jeopardize the only shot they have at winning this race, and in doing so hurt races up and down the ballot, you're not alone. But all anyone has to do is file three forms and they're in the primary. It's shameful, unethical, disparaging. But is it illegal? Only time (and the lawyers) will tell. [Send a message to potential spoiler candidates and their recruiters that Rebekah is the only choice for Florida's First Congressional District by contributing to the campaign today](https://www.rebekahjonescampaign.com/so/f5NvqHdIM/c?w=vZ-gD6EXHCwqaznBAKe-K_AM00-DyxX_LcUec74r1dQ.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUubnVtZXJvLmFpL2NvbnRyaWJ1dGUvUmViZWthaC1Kb25lcy1Db25ncmVzcyIsInIiOiIwNmRkOGE1My05NGFhLTQwY2EtMGQxZi00ZDMyMzRiYTIyMmMiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiI3ZTdhODdlMS03MDQzLTQxNzktYjgzOC01Y2E4OTIwZmFhZDcifQ)!

This public release discusses recent news and events related to the 2022 elections, as well as campaign updates and plans for January-February 2022, a statement about spoiler candidates, and calls for , and . News and Recent Events Election 2022: Rebekah Jones vs (Matt Gaetz?)Matt Gaetz' ex-girlfriend against him in front of a grandy jury in Orlando related to charges of obstruction and sex trafficking minors. While an indictment may come soon, Gaetz has resisted pressure from his own party to step down since last fall, when. It's not clear if an indictment will force his hand, but he continues to campaign and raise hundreds of thousands of dollars for his legal defense either way. Further marred by his own bad behavior, Gaetz attempted to divert attention away from his pedophilia by , to protect workers and attendees from rising COVID-19 cases, hospitalizations and deaths, by writing Florida's Criminal-in-Chief, DeSantis, who approved the concert's rules. The download spiral continued from there, with Gaetz announcing that he "" his membership to a Capital Hill club in Washington D.C. for complying with the city's new vaccine mandates (he wouldn't be allowed in without it, anyways). Gaetz has no problem complying with mask mandates on flights - I know because I was on one of his flights from DC to Pensacola last month. But his antics aren't getting him the attention he's used to, with most coverage coming from tabloids like the Daily Mail, or his own podcast. Still, he's fundraising off his insanity, which is why we need to show him how absurd he is by outpacing him in this quarter's fundraising goals.--> <\-- Election 2022: Desantis unravelingA long-simmering feud between Trump and Desantis , with the two publicly shaming each other for their respective failed leadership. Someone on Trump's team pointed out that no one gets under Desantis' skin like I do, so I got a surprise message about how Trump could endorse me just to spite DeSantis. Never a fan of Gaetz, Trump wouldn't think twice about kicking Gaetz to the curb and making a comment as honest and simple as "The way Ron Desantis went after Rebekah Jones and her family was wrong." And Trump would be right to do it - Desantis' obsession with my success has been well-documented over the last year-and-a-half. Even the mention about his illegal raid of my home, my firing for defending science, and his coercing Twitter to suspend my account... and Desantis starts yelling at reporters. The man who emails about one female scientist 12 times a day every single day for a year straight can hardly contain himself when publicly confronted with his sickness. The spat between Trump and Desantis intensified when Roger Stone revealed that Desantis lied about his whereabouts over a two-week period in December when he went AWOL as COVID-19 spun out of control and a). During the MIA period in December, Desantis' spokesmonster first claimed the family went on vacation, then another spokesperson claimed he was the hospital with his wife, who is undergoing treatment for breast cancer. According to Stone, Desantis was actually with a Newsmax reporter at the time. Roger Stone claims to have video evidence, but after Matt Gaetz' unhinged "" conference, you just can't trust the GOP to deliver on the video proof the way I always have. A flurry of click-bait came out of the Governor's office following the news of the affair, including the firing of the Orange County Director of Health at DOH f in an email that landed him on administrative leave. And despite Omicron rendering Desantis' prized antibody treatments useless, he , lining the pockets of his largest donors. This follows DOH Director Ladapo's public statement . Desantis also require who feel uncomfortable learning about slavery and the Civil War. "Surely that's a misrepresentation of what he's proposing?" you might think/ask. I wish. Here's the bill's language, from a : "A bill that would ban public schools and private companies from making white people "feel discomfort, guilt, anguish, or any other form of psychological distress" when being taught or trained about discrimination in the nation's past passed out of the State Senate Education Committee on a party-line vote Tuesday." And if Desantis' dangling of derivative disputations didn't cover up the rising cases and deaths, the affair, and the feud with Trump, perhaps his . Or the of the constitutional rights of Floridians. The silver lining? The world seems to have finally woken up to the danger Desantis poses to our communities, state and nation, rightfully (and finally) calling him out for what he is: .\-----And while all of the melodrama and useless busyness unfolded, I was meeting with local leaders to ensure we were representing everyone in our district fairly, providing resources to veterans trying to access their benefits and other local services, making progress on expanding and diversifying our campaign staff, planning out the next few months to win this election, and taking care of my son, who has been in the hospital since last Friday. I'm starting to think I'm a rare breed of future Congress members who aren't engulfed in conspiracy, criminal activity, and cronyism every day of the week. Some call it refreshing, while I say it should be the new standard. Campaign Activities ❄️January❄️ Planning for the 2022 election kicked off for us with ambitious goals and aggressive outreach - which is why we need your support to meet our benchmarks over the next six weeks! Did you know? Contributions totaling less than $200.01 are not itemized with the FEC and the identity of those donors is kept confidential. The maximum amount any individual can donate between now and August 23, 2022 is $2,900 (total). While COVID-19 put a wrinkle in our event plans this month, we’re resuming in-person operations in February, assuming this most recent wave of the virus begins to recede by then. Most of our planned public events in February will be held outdoors. ❤️February ❤️ Black History Month \- We're celebrating the people and places that make up Northwest Florida’s African American history, including our famous (Gen. Daniel “Chappie” James) and lesser-known (Willie “Blue” Waitman) black community leaders. In tandem with original artwork commissioned with local artist Carter J, we’ll be adding a storymap to our website where members of the community can submit the people, places and stories that inspire them during Black History Month. Valentine’s Day Meet and Greet \- We’ll be celebrating Valentine’s day all weekend, with meet and greets scheduled across Northwest Florida. Events will be announced over the next few weeks. Plus...Volunteers wanted for Valentine’s Day postcard writing to voters on Feb. 9-10! We’ll be outside at my house on the beach! Cards, stamps, food, drinks, etc. will be provided! Email us at to volunteer! ❤️ Mardi Gras Parades and Balls-If there’s a Mardi Gras parade in Northwest Florida this month, we’ll be there. For the ones that overlap, we'll have teams at both!Krewe of Seville Ball, Feb. 5Krewe of Warriors Ball, Feb. 11Milton Parade, Feb. 12Ft. Walton Beach Parade, Feb. 13Pensacola Beach Kids and Pets Parade, Feb. 19Navarre Beach Mardi Gras Parade, Feb. 19Mardi Gras Ball at the Pennsylvania Yacht Club, Feb. 25Cordova Mardi Gras Ball, Feb. 25Pensacola Mardi Gras Parade, Feb. 26Krewe of Wrecks Parade, Pensacola Beach, Feb. 27Pawdi Gras Destin, Feb. 27 Other events/press:Viewpoint with Seeta and Friends, radio show Feb. 7 at 12:00 PM ETSea Turtle Conservation & Community Sustainability Workshop, Feb. 8Pensacon, Feb. 18-20, 2022Gallery Night (Pensacon), Feb. 18, 5-10 PMBeaches Activists Movement of Brevard County Speaking Event, Feb. 24 6 PM CT Polling: Near the end of February, we’ll conduct another large-scale poll of where voters stand with the issues, with the incumbent and his behavior, and on Rebekah’s favorability heading into prime-time election season. On the ground: Petition signing, talking to voters, door-to-door canvassing and more! Volunteers needed to help us reach more of our neighbors! Every contribution helps us mail more letters, attend more events, schedule more meet and greets, and continue to engage with our community in meaningful waysAre you a registered Florida voter? Send us a ballot petition! to download a petition with instructions! We need all petitions sent to us, which we then verify with the state, by April 2022! Digital signatures are not accepted by the state of Florida at this time. \----- On the home front: Local Party Politics and Statement on Spoiler Candidates We heard a lot about Republican in Florida during the last election cycle - one in south Florida even . Spoiler candidates with similar sounding names or identical last names who don't campaign are the most obvious example of "spoiler candidate recruiting," a practice that I was pleasantly surprised to find out is illegal. But what is it called when a small group of people pay a candidate who doesn't live in the district, cannot win a primary, isn't from the state, can't or doesn't fundraise, and serves only as a name on the ballot to keep away endorsements and financing from larger groups? Is that not also a spoiler candidate scheme, even if the two candidates' names aren't the same? Aside from the ethical issues present when those involved in this scheme hold official titles or positions within the party they're sabotaging (that should disqualify them immediately, right?), what are the legal consequences of engaging in this activity, if any? That's a question my attorneys and I have been trying to figure out these last two months. I've been put through a crash course on local corruption in party politics this past year, and it's shocking to me how small minded people with an ounce power will intentionally sabotage their own community, country, party, even their own ideals, out of pettiness - and it absolutely happens within both political parties. Refuse to kiss the ring of a self-important wealthy socialite? Expect a spoiler candidate to be on the ballot to keep the national party from giving you money, or to keep you from getting major endorsements where the by-laws prohibit them from endorsing before a primary. Become a cross-over candidate with appeal in parties and with NPAs, while becoming the first candidate in 30 years polling within 6 points of the GOP incumbent, with a national platform and international praise for your work and advocacy? Be ready for a small group of people who can't win elections themselves to recruit someone to run against you. If they know they can't beat you, they'll just lie, cheat and sabotage every other candidate on the ballot to punish you for the crime of not needing their support, not wanting the strings that come with their support, and refusing their ultimatums. For me, this came from a very small and isolated group of people that have never won their own races for public office, and found someone who doesn't even live in the district anymore, who moved to the state for the first time in 2020, and admittedly has no chance of winning, to list their name on the ballot for the sole purpose of preventing me from getting full funding and party support, as well as major endorsements. Primary elections in Florida are August 23, 2022, giving candidates a measly 11 weeks to bring in major groups and organizers for fundraising (unless you take dark money before then, which I have not done and will not do). I believe living in the district you want to represent should be a requirement, but it's not. I believe campaign financing should be transparent, but it's not. The second item I plan to fix in Congress by supporting legislation that will overturn Citizens United. Because if you don't live here, work here, send your kids to school here, you can't fully understand the challenges we face as families here every single day -- even absent of a pandemic. We need to normalize electing people who care more about our communities than they do about political favoritism, grabbing power, or grifting off the bloated political infrastructure in this country. I'm the only candidate here who has roots in Florida \- my grandparents are from (and still live in) Florida; my dad even went to Boca High; my son grew up attending Florida's public schools (and still does); and my daughter was born right here in the Panhandle. I went to college in Florida, worked for the state of Florida, and my husband still works for the state of Florida as a scientist - going on six years now. I'm the only one who has fought for Floridians, and risked everything in doing so - including my job, safety and freedom. I'm the only candidate who has polled within striking distance of the GOP incumbent here since 1992, and at the last poll in October, I was only trailing by 6 points. Even the Cook Political Index updated the first district to reflect recent changes we've been making on the ground, slicing off 10 points from its R+ rating. I'm the only candidate who didn't grow up with money, who works with our neighbors to get resources and services they need, and is hitting the pavement every single day to win this election. I'm the only candidate actively recruiting honest, hard-working community leaders to fight for every office in my community so that no corrupt school board member, county commissioner or state legislature runs unopposed. And I do all of this while taking care of my family, managing my data systems, filming a documentary, suing the state, and continuing my work as a science advocate in the media. If you're wondering why on Earth any Democrat would be engaged in activities that could jeopardize the only shot they have at winning this race, and in doing so hurt races up and down the ballot, you're not alone. But all anyone has to do is file three forms and they're in the primary. It's shameful, unethical, disparaging. But is it illegal? Only time (and the lawyers) will tell. ! | | Print, sign, date and mail a ballot petition to us! | | Make a contribution to our campaign, or buy merchandise from our ActBlue store! | | | | Share on social | | | Paid for by Rebekah Jones Campaign | | | You've received this email because you are a subscriber of . If you feel you received it by mistake or wish to unsubscribe, please _.

name: MichaelBennet.com
subject: Wealthy corporate CEOs are trying to flip Colorado
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| Friend —

One of Michael's GOP challengers raised $1 million last quarter. Much of that came from his own pocket — **he’s using his own money in an attempt to buy this seat, and has already spent it on misleading attack ads across the state.**

We're proud to be running a campaign driven by grassroots supporters, but we're up against wealthy opponents that could inject tons of cash into this race when Republican grassroots enthusiasm is low. They don’t need to appeal to everyday folks to get their message out there when they can just spend wads of cash to buy this seat.

$1 million is a lot in a race like this, especially when combined with the more than $75 million Mitch McConnell’s raised to flip seats like this.

When candidates like these, who don’t reflect our values, make their way to Congress, the result is inaction on the issues that matter most to folks like you.

That’s not Michael. He’s fighting hard to build on the tax cuts he achieved for working families, to fight climate change, and create more good-paying jobs. Grassroots supporters like you power this campaign, not federal lobbyists or corporate PACs.

[**Can you pitch in today to keep Colorado blue and our Senate leadership working for everyday Coloradans?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/5190?t=6&akid=1143%2E878179%2ELOq_1x) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $5 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/5186?t=7&akid=1143%2E878179%2ELOq_1x)

name: BetoORourke.com
subject: Update on your assignment in Anderson County
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
We came up with a plan to work together and make sure we fight back everywhere.
#
We just got some numbers back on Greg Abbott’s statewide TV ad blitz. He’s spending over $2.3 million in total on ads — and running ads in _every single_ Texas media market.
Together, these markets span every inch of Texas. So we came up with a plan to work together and make sure that after tomorrow’s state-mandated fundraising deadline, we’ll report numbers that show we’re ready to fight back against Abbott’s spending _everywhere._
**We recently assigned you to sponsor Anderson County** , where more than 28,856 people are registered to vote, as part of our 254-County Mobilization Plan.
We now know that Greg Abbott has already spent $516,448 in the greater Dallas media market where Anderson County is located, so we’re setting a goal for you and your fellow sponsors to match his spending in your region. It’s an ambitious goal, but we can get it done together.
[Will you chip in any amount right now to help us reach Texans in Anderson County before Greg Abbott’s ads do?](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/51765?refcode=ema20220119-172350&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&County=Anderson&Voters=28%2C856&t=1&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c) _Every 50 cents you give will allow us to reach at least one voter!_
[_⇢ Make a $25 donation to reach 50 voters in Anderson County._](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/51766?refcode=ema20220119-172350-el&County=Anderson&Voters=28%2C856&t=2&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c)
[_⇢ Make a $50 donation to reach 100 voters in Anderson County._](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/51767?refcode=ema20220119-172350-el&County=Anderson&Voters=28%2C856&t=3&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c)
[_⇢ Make a $100 donation to reach 200 voters in Anderson County._](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/51768?refcode=ema20220119-172350-el&County=Anderson&Voters=28%2C856&t=4&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c)
[_⇢ Make a $250 donation to reach 500 voters in Anderson County._](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/51769?refcode=ema20220119-172350-el&County=Anderson&Voters=28%2C856&t=5&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c)
**[FIGHT BACK: DONATE NOW](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/51765?refcode=ema20220119-172350&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&County=Anderson&Voters=28%2C856&t=6&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c)**
The secret to winning in a state as big and diverse as Texas is to not write anyone off. No county is too small, too Republican, or too anything for us to compete for their votes.
So while Abbott drops $2.3 million (and counting) on blasting ads across the state, we’re going to do something way better: raise $2.3 million together in grassroots contributions to staff up and organize in all 254 counties.
**But to reach a goal this ambitious, we need every county’s grassroots team to participate.**[Please, will you chip in as a Anderson sponsor to help raise $516,448 and match Abbott’s spending in the greater Dallas media market? Every donation will translate into on-the-ground organizing to elect Beto as our next Governor. ](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/51765?refcode=ema20220119-172350&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&County=Anderson&Voters=28%2C856&t=7&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c)
There are so many Texans out there who are looking for a reason to join our campaign. Thank you for considering a donation to reach the 28,856 registered voters of Anderson County.
Let’s do this,
Beto for Texas

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bft-em-footer?refcode=em20220119-172350-footer&t=8&refcode2=172350_444211_AeTP4c&akid=172350%2E444211%2EAeTP4c)**

name: Josh Mandel
subject: Should Congress be able to trade stocks?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Josh A joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Citizens for Josh Mandel, Inc
body:
Take the poll today Peter, As one of my Top Supporters, **I need to know where you stand today**. Congress has long built a reputation for insider trading run amok. disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JACK BALLARD FOR MONTANA
body:
One in every five Montana residents is a registered hunter. So, hunting issues are Montana issues. But Matt Rosendale doesn’t seem to think so. In fac | | | [](http://links.ballardformontana.com/u/click?_t=01481013522b4e28a72d84f828a757ef&_m=9d6bb022ad2245ae8048743d3f170059&_e=VFFSW9Gy2s_fpWoEIsj8V6gAkivkQXzF_28vb9yiupeq6tSFTsa-IZ-CV09HvE879QOE95cYYABdUEoOlM5FAqSIb8suG7Opar4NqtDPIIX2EegI6fZvykkN25iIuPQP_KiNyXBxR-WjfsSLkqgm6zLkDSB13r6ZJQxGq369psHng0fJKio4K2_KL50_9uTT9m1OcGE7Q1BReRbKwSJ-BWG2mi5H6xdmu7b4g5dKJsGirTX9G78zY_Q2kvlzAt9TFmrbnm1kkwmea49fHCTeLalD1EGa3WoJ4eqdV7L1QKp9rf5WGCDdzxQQVvgGWk97eAsPCn5zS6WJNRCJ6hW_FN58kq9thvY99tFMhB5OjxdtHGaDEs8F7E7IeAxIR2W52cG3HNvLCdL_4xB43UPNYzb_dciHKUsjZ3Yy12kbw19VLltUSw01hB34eNtQi-LGsHH6CjRIWyqy0H1PSeSj548uKvGYxSCKjaLHhL6ML3SJapLHnBBmf6S7ByYSYDmO) Peter, One in every five Montana residents is a registered hunter. So, hunting issues are Montana issues. But Matt Rosendale doesn’t seem to think so. **In fact, he couldn’t even bother to show up for a bipartisan Congressional vote that directly affects the health and safety of the hunters in his state.** CWD is a fatal neurological condition that’s spreading rapidly in our country’s deer, elk, and moose. Meats must be tested for CWD before they can be consumed to ensure they’re safe. Last month, Congressional Democrats and Republicans cosponsored The Chronic Wasting Disease Research and Management Act which passed on an overwhelming 393-33 vote. Matt Rosendale did not show up for a vote that arguably affects his constituents more than any other state’s population. **His people needed him and he failed to even show up.** Jack Ballard is one of the many hunters directly affected by the CWD Research and Management Act. You can ALWAYS count on him to show up for votes that affect our community. **[Will you chip in $5 to help us elect him in clean_body: One in every five Montana residents is a registered hunter. So, hunting issues are Montana issues. But Matt Rosendale doesn’t seem to think so. In fac | | | Peter, One in every five Montana residents is a registered hunter. So, hunting issues are Montana issues. But Matt Rosendale doesn’t seem to think so. In fact, he couldn’t even bother to show up for a bipartisan Congressional vote that directly affects the health and safety of the hunters in his state. CWD is a fatal neurological condition that’s spreading rapidly in our country’s deer, elk, and moose. Meats must be tested for CWD before they can be consumed to ensure they’re safe. Last month, Congressional Democrats and Republicans cosponsored The Chronic Wasting Disease Research and Management Act which passed on an overwhelming 393-33 vote. Matt Rosendale did not show up for a vote that arguably affects his constituents more than any other state’s population. His people needed him and he failed to even show up. Jack Ballard is one of the many hunters directly affected by the CWD Research and Management Act. You can ALWAYS count on him to show up for votes that affect our community. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | ---|--- | | Time after time, Rosendale proves that he doesn’t have the best interests of Montanans at heart. subject: Read Charlie’s comments submitted to the Legislative Reapportionment Commission
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Charlie For Governor**
body:
| | | | January 19, 2021 For Immediate Release:

Contact: team@charlieforgovernor.com
717-823-1806
---
| [ ](https://charlieforgovernor.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7f407e0a8a35bc04db6508d81&id=06d46b29a5&e=df21354de5)
---
| Read Charlie’s comments submitted to the Legislative Reapportionment Commission stating his opposition to the proposed House of Representatives map. Charlie says that the commission chair Mark Nordenberg tipped the scales in favor of the Democrats. For example, 14 House Republicans have been put into the same district to run against each other while just two House Democrats have been.

I write in opposition to the Pennsylvania House of Representatives map proposed by Chairman Mark Nordenberg and favored by the Democrats on the Legislative Reapportionment Commission (LRC).

This map not only isn’t fair it was drawn to help House Democrats take back the majority. Don’t let Nordenberg try and fool us into thinking that his process has been a fair and bipartisan exercise.

The Princeton Gerrymandering Project gives his map an “F” for competitiveness. The citizens mapping tool Dave’s Redistricting App estimates that the Nordenberg map will give House Democrats a legislative majority of 106 seats, dramatically more than their current total of 90 seats.

How does the Nordenberg map go from 90 Democrats to 106? By tipping the scales. Under Nordenberg’s map, 14 House Republicans have been put into the same district to run against each other while just two House Democrats have been.

Let’s not kid ourselves, this fall when LRC commission member Joanna McClinton was asked at the PA Press Club how she plans to get back in the majority she had a one word answer: “Reapportionment.”

Since they haven’t been able to achieve a Democratic majority at the ballot box, the goal of McClinton, Nordenberg and the Democrats is to use a partisan gerrymandering process to tip the scales to accomplish their goal.

The Nordenberg map is unconstitutional. The Pennsylvania Constitution states “unless absolutely necessary, no county, city, incorporated town, borough, township or ward shall be divided” in creating a legislative district.

Under the Nordenberg map Harrisburg, Lancaster, State College, Reading, Allentown and Scranton are all divided or “split.” Is it “absolutely necessary” to split these six cites and why does Nordenberg split them?

The simple answer is this is a flagrant attempt by Nordenberg to spread Democratic voters across more districts to win more seats.

Read his full his comments **[HERE](https://charlieforgovernor.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7f407e0a8a35bc04db6508d81&id=85441b0377&e=df21354de5)**.
---

body:
CPAC is one of the largest and most influential organizations in the conservative movement. They’ve helped elect conservatives across the country for Friend, CPAC is one of the largest and most influential organizations in the conservative movement. They’ve helped elect conservatives across the country for nearly five decades, and were a major part of launching the movements that landed both Ronald Reagan and Donald Trump in the White House. We wanted to make sure you were one of the first to know: **CPAC has ENDORSED Derek Schmidt for Governor!** [](http://links.schmidtforkansas.com/u/click?_t=4eb8bd5dcca54a0b993a2eb1eb42ba73&_m=396ae61aaf6b4780b89d20d727fb2957&_e=uU6BQ0kYI-V4BwWbMMI81F0DjYN3coGkJnWMonXPnBiTti1kStm2j5UnOsC2Q73M4Yy0feZRm5pqz3vSkHkSFfniAxzf2vNJ-LeAKB63I1aLES2wUMec8dzGyLA9NRDXVoHBVaNUP4DvC-Z-NqMqdGVeqsuwxH2lukvWRgV682VKno4cHN7ahbN_ZpknaEapJfr5touKoPitVFRDeAXD_piCyF3VISchVchKjHenFKdFFVUwWJfEIzJGoWY3S8pwCXq0Kccvggz00y5yo_83sd-qs_nN9grNOLKilOfQwuptN0Mnh0BO8BfhTWT2AMbBfC2ahb2KDvD1VgzFyoeC__pjBvEpTOa0JeRr_WXNq3qr1zZ1okkTpwENvwVCCni5kmzSOK5uESP30Q1uCgLVIhXvben0jRGuIqZiUNDdzxDwoO1VOrOTE0N9XHH-RMmvBsoOgp0Pgk3YrBNlpBifg1jrz_2FnMRIqKGKfu1fKdA%3D) Here’s what CPAC Chairman Matt Schlapp - and fellow Kansan - had to say about Derek: _“CPAC endorses Derek Schmidt to be the next Governor of Kansas. _ _“Kansas Attorney General Derek Schmidt has long been a champion of service to the people of Kansas and will certainly continue as Governor. Derek has spent the last decade fighting for the rights of Kansas’ citizens and standing as a bulwark of conservative values against federal government overreach. Kansas and the nation are in constant and dire need of the leadership supported by the conservative and patriotic ideals that Derek Schmidt has provided as attorney general. His constant and proven track record of defending and enforcing constitutional rights demonstrate that as governor, Derek will stand strong in the representation of his people against the forces of federal tyranny. CPAC is proud to endorse Derek Schmidt and we call for all citizens of Kansas to support his candidacy for Governor in this election.”_ **– CPAC Chairman Matt Schlapp** This is a massive endorsement, friend, and we’re really hoping we can ensure you'll stand with CPAC and join team Schmidt. [**JOIN TEAM SCHMIDT**](http://links.schmidtforkansas.com/u/click?_t=4eb8bd5dcca54a0b993a2eb1eb42ba73&_m=396ae61aaf6b4780b89d20d727fb2957&_e=uU6BQ0kYI-V4BwWbMMI81F0DjYN3coGkJnWMonXPnBiTti1kStm2j5UnOsC2Q73M4Yy0feZRm5pqz3vSkHkSFfniAxzf2vNJ-LeAKB63I1aLES2wUMec8dzGyLA9NRDXVoHBVaNUP4DvC-Z-NqMqdGVeqsuwxH2lukvWRgV682VKno4cHN7ahbN_ZpknaEapJfr5touKoPitVFRDeAXD_piCyF3VISchVchKjHenFKdFFVUwWJfEIzJGoWY3S8pwCXq0Kccvggz00y5yo_83sXiwsW3DBUK8sIr0kIr1_LyAQB4CGa2tOFZ13M1YE3ku11VYepfQEsUeYiinzTBNVMmaLBcWUDJWIjvuXDQZG8wyVqkhbtfQf_G8Cc3Q8UbbvFv75rJmBupLNwD1jAHrUfyPes99nitoP_cZtEl5eTehbf4SIbibxuJIYAxNBBQWZkAfWXeQvGjVGKsNQL07Nh0vg05dZVEhxyb_PkLH4zY%3D) [** WIN WITH DEREK**](http://links.schmidtforkansas.com/u/click?_t=4eb8bd5dcca54a0b993a2eb1eb42ba73&_m=396ae61aaf6b4780b89d20d727fb2957&_e=uU6BQ0kYI-V4BwWbMMI81F0DjYN3coGkJnWMonXPnBiTti1kStm2j5UnOsC2Q73M4Yy0feZRm5pqz3vSkHkSFfniAxzf2vNJ-LeAKB63I1aLES2wUMec8dzGyLA9NRDXVoHBVaNUP4DvC-Z-NqMqdGVeqsuwxH2lukvWRgV682VKno4cHN7ahbN_ZpknaEapJfr5touKoPitVFRDeAXD_piCyF3VISchVchKjHenFKdFFVUwWJfEIzJGoWY3S8pwCXq0Kccvggz00y5yo_83sdxcec6OM9EF4NY4tB2I8263juup69lbhboYSsE_SCf8fLY1_A8Vg-EpLbcjOmbHMV_EMUMMG5WBH-SuhXIfqsXKeOKsn8i6Ee0CnxSGV90x3TG7FHwFofQ-WGKeB2t0Ld9pZOYVNIyFMswA_NccWyaFcpUFj6ZDb92tFwqpGVbKx9SInJk9PIYgylh3FdfUYOY0TGrNqn55BpgE6e7C0no%3D) Derek is ready to step up and lead. name: =?utf-8?Q?Bill=20Olson?=
subject: Biden messed up COVID
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WILLIAM OLSON FOR CONGRESS
body:
It beat him |
---
| | [ ](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=8f7dfa7e37&e=afa21b7626)
---
| Hi Peter,

Remember when Biden famously claimed, "I’m going to shut down the virus"?

Well, surprise surprise, he was LYING!

**My name is Bill Olson, and I am a Christian and a combat vet [running to reclaim Florida's 9th district](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=307b1afeea&e=afa21b7626), a swing district south of Orlando. This is a targeted district by the NRCC and I can take it back!**

The National Review sums this up well:

_When Joe Biden pledged, “I’m going to shut down the virus,” he thought all he would have to do is tell everyone to wear masks for 100 days and finish the vaccine rollout started under President Trump. Alas, the task is more complicated — and the president’s favorite scapegoat, the unvaccinated, are a smaller and smaller percentage of the public. As of today, 86.4 percent of American adults have at least one shot. We don’t have a massive wave of Omicron cases, or a shortage of tests, because of the unvaccinated.

Omicron is different. It’s much, much less likely to kill you, and if you’re vaccinated and boosted, it’s likely going to feel like a winter cold. But we’re seeing that even a non-fatal virus can cause major problems throughout the country by keeping workers home sick.

Unfortunately, Biden doesn’t have many more tools or ideas. The virus is so contagious, it can’t really be contained. Cases are exploding even in the places with the strictest masking policies, making the old masking debates moot. He’s urging schools to remain open, but not publicly criticizing unions that want to close them._
---
| [ ](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=5eb059f78a&e=afa21b7626)
---
| Anyone tried to get a COVID-19 test this week? If so I’m sure you share my frustration. I would say it’s like “pulling teeth“, but I’m pretty sure it’s much easier to get your teeth pulled!

If the federal government can’t HELP administer a simple test where they stick a Q-tip up your nose, what makes them think they can control global climate change!?

They can’t, not even spending the trillions of dollars that they are demanding in Build Back Better. They are trying to undo civilization.

When you get elected, you’re in charge. _If things go well, you get the praise._

**_If things don’t go well, you get the blame_**! You either provide results, or you do not!

President Biden and Congressman Darren Soto have failed to protect the people of our district! **It’s obvious they don’t have a clue what to do to contain this virus.**

President Trump handed the Democrats three vaccines (such that they are) and President Biden promised us that he would “ Shut down the virus”.

Instead, more people have died under Biden and Soto, than last year under Trump- though not in the Free State of Florida. Yet the media is silent!

_Well, I’m not gonna be silent! I will hold our government officials to account!_

Soto and Biden have to go!

**Darren Soto had his chance to lead, protect, and represent......he failed!**
---
| [ ](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=ee86e3fc88&e=afa21b7626)
---
| **_What do you have to lose?

I care about the people of our district! These are my neighbors, my friends.

No government official can truthfully say that they can stop a pandemic. That’s just hyperbole, and that leader needs to be replaced!_**

Why hasn’t Congressman Soto spoken out and urged the President to act?

Cricket Sounds......as our neighbors die....

Our neighbors need a voice in Washington DC. We need to cut through the bureaucracy.

It didn’t take long for the swamp to swallow Congressman Soto. He’s only been our rep for three terms, but unfortunately he’s neck deep, and sinking fast!

**The swamp won’t swallow me- 27 years of faithful service in uniform taught me how to lead even when the going gets tough!**

[I got more votes here in 2020 than any Republican ever had, because I stood up and fought for our freedoms!](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=32c1ba335a&e=afa21b7626) Biden is just an incompetent Communist. I'll fight him every day!

Help me stand up to Biden and his incompetence! [Help me stop all of Biden's agenda!](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=97a4311e3b&e=afa21b7626)

[Thank you for your support!](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=3d59c9a777&e=afa21b7626)
---
| [ ](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=de65cc8859&e=afa21b7626)
---
| Bill Olson
Combat Veteran of Multiple Wars
Supports President Trump
Not a Career Politician
Hard Core Conservative Fighter
---
| [ ](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=17c6b3c7bc&e=afa21b7626)
---
| P.S. I am in the fight of my life against the Democrat machine here in Florida. [Will you contribute before the end of this month to help me reach my goal?](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=1eb0b58a0e&e=afa21b7626)
---
| |

PAID FOR BY WILLIAM OLSON FOR CONGRESS

William Olson for Congress242 Kendal WayDavenport, FL 33837-4542

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=a9a8add464&e=afa21b7626&c=8d106d50ed) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://billolsonfl.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=d46f7bf5f11f241ec4a0954ff&id=a9a8add464&e=afa21b7626&c=8d106d50ed).

name: Odessa Kelly
subject: I wish I were joking.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ODESSA FOR CONGRESS** ](https://u1584542
body:
At his son’s high school basketball game, Tennessee State Rep. Jeremy Faison got so angry at a referee that he tried to fight — and pants — the guy. | | | [](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xQsZ_uHW9ME9h2VPudEgVBlT0rAEcoX_uBPlqDgEOfEQIhz_Vu9SFmd68KrwuQ3aKi7nPZoMC1hdvv-aBtd_WQhF_HHr7R5OkEDrc_ZkrefNsrGEBGswqDddjhUUq7yQjjRbO0M0K3x_WX8op5_lLTseIul8F40FkzHU_qVmGmDD53U6mNSKSqVuITA6iZQhwC_6fI9SDXeANXbaYC9bPOFd3qwl5U5KiSnHdT1gbTFXaZm25ha7GGdAIBCnAfIDv1GG_CTCdc9lqBT9bd8wc2so0xn3kKxa0gVWr1MEmnYhmmvvo4utrFuBr52VhSQ7m_2fSqgV36NJYrS1sHVOMc/3iu/eGflUIU6T5-xpPTI-siflA/h0/HJDaPD8xQmZEP4_mNGNl6vZbFVl0vp6XQCtLQ3Ko4EA)
| Before I get into the pantsing nonsense, hear me out — right now, Middle Tennessee is in the fight of its life against new racist, anti-democratic gerrymandering. [**_Will you rush a donation to our campaign to help us call out this injustice and fight back? »_**](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xWIurlfE4kaqpZPqSu1iEiNtFnEqROArqDSm80XKABQm53aa9CkuX8N_6szO56jGuGSxkPQ4E6NtiK6vZrS3QPyjRBPs2hQ4p9nqoyMPYv6JA7Ltvfp_bU6SOiMPejix_mZiZ_WkIyFIqcFNwSb1PRgAd8LTgaVaUQzS4QZU6PydTB2TYMoBZziE8QRWjsfv3qgfrahk2Dvi-WTDIvbekQN2YwGawMHirNmhqS6rXT_e5TxEPOIevVqSzF_RakwS1ClbUfodhs4f6I3qRjKsGGdvKXWNBecXpNrTCJru75F0Svc5iW-u_PQ7TXuR5fEoJrwHcQnIKqtFSRVf5oMm0G/3iu/eGflUIU6T5-xpPTI-siflA/h1/5k13MnqSSCN7Jdx74geJoGOvNQ5lEpOl9YxRJBBXNQg)
---
Peter,
Last week, at his son’s high school basketball game, Tennessee State Rep. Jeremy Faison got so angry at a referee that he tried to fight — and pants — the guy.********
That’s right, pants. I wish I were joking.

[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xWIurlfE4kaqpZPqSu1iEiNtFnEqROArqDSm80XKABQm53aa9CkuX8N_6szO56jGs_CdowSUcwAOIYZE_TMQyBB9YUSTH6U9fbvmWzkOGAw4bMpAx7TdqhIbGgndsdtv9M_HSaplxmkgU1FU317I-xUFafJY1N78AYdSEm8m9ZF6F2Vy-E6mpFzjJektx-4mShvBjkNKPThSc7cpQYKh_n-IPOT5naqiiidhOxV2QDsYJTyvzF8bckthSsJMEHgLsVVOscc6SzXyR0m9BvDwwpVjeKVzBHI9Jo8lSQ4c9MD9lmXMGCKnjJ6b91FIOmlWUwONfwj7sBJw78C-Fs2xJk/3iu/eGflUIU6T5-xpPTI-siflA/h2/Ee9PtBMHbow3BspPTcuv4p8BccjL09YTgFra0zoJNJM)
Not only is this clown the House GOP Chair, he’s a member of the House Redistricting Committee. **That** **means he was one of the legislators who voted last week to approve the new Congressional maps to break apart our progressive stronghold here in Tennessee.**
Peter, I’ll be honest. Watching lawmakers like this man wield power and purposefully dilute the voices of Black, brown, and working class people in Nashville has me deeply frustrated, saddened, and angry.
This is no longer a democracy where everyone’s voices are heard. This is the new Jim Crow, where racist politicians will do anything to erase us and strip power from us. **But I refuse to back down in the face of white supremacy, and if you’re with me, I need your help right now.**

[**_We’re working to mobilize thousands of Tennessee voters to fight back against gerrymandering — but we’re running short on resources. Will you chip in whatever you can today to ensure we can reach as many people as possible?_**](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xWIurlfE4kaqpZPqSu1iEiNtFnEqROArqDSm80XKABQm53aa9CkuX8N_6szO56jGu0vqJk6OlTYUyUTSwDAI5ZHG2SIOhs3PshMu2wLI7to-5ym6zRgCsSBnahUG0fJOR_Dwm2Hkhu964fvBoyCvDd5l9BFzYB_jZ4gczhMlnAZPV0lr-YnbiwhR3BlHUgKvCqgphMdA7ZMXjlheNStnzBPKOiTz1SNwJSdJCAU5MpVxSJ3xZMamADJGwK4R2Iqlvh4CodWlkazWfjM7ccA9ioOa0ZRDjya_DYEHkb-TY8vuMXzo5TYxtNEb86DiAj7QQ-R7pUXlm3oXs1AwjXO1oH/3iu/eGflUIU6T5-xpPTI-siflA/h3/JH-zkfSXuRGwo9hx7qj3pzSF-mn_JoIXFO-L62FfWaU)
[Contribute »](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xWIurlfE4kaqpZPqSu1iEiNtFnEqROArqDSm80XKABQm53aa9CkuX8N_6szO56jGvCq7FwIH8ia-h-cyZS1xywFNNEXgACkrPowli9YLkV7s25-3hc0iICFuDDQlO7rfGbxaN4GCNlic_yhGDtliRS_US8KN4z_a30Dbv8PZzdjoTpw0qnpROYwOflo8m7ECmmrYbbuEKMoq58an3ia-rsOzS20szFnH0WMJjbr1W1H7-nc6gJIQ1z_s-3bqyJHgjT0eTXU2wpnSoYDsSe1A-MlCgd9KmeXm_qW4ccBWuoegW7omo6aPUTH75NPnnm7AgdswezA45aJvPNfEoV7Jv-/3iu/eGflUIU6T5-xpPTI-siflA/h4/f3X3Bws5YZ_F8y_8ooUxlR_RlGZNk5btArRheAVVpIM)
Thanks for your support,

Odessa Kelly

body:
We delivered over 20,000 signatures to Lansing today Peter | | | [ ](https://GarrettForMichigan.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=db0473a6037c1be0f2423e4fd&id=6e2b8cc533&e=fe4b890356)
---
| **Today was EPIC, Peter.**

We delivered over 20,000 signatures to get We the People's campaign for Governor on the Republican Primary ballot in August.

Our movement is leveling up - **and we're gaining some real momentum.**
---
| [ ](https://GarrettForMichigan.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=db0473a6037c1be0f2423e4fd&id=15d5afbd71&e=fe4b890356)
---
| The grassroots army we're building is up against the establishment's deep pockets and special interests, and we are relying on the financial support from patriotic citizens like you to win this Republican Primary and take on Gretchen Whitmer in November.

_**We're not slowing down, folks. **_

Our team is celebrating today's momentous accomplishment by setting a __fundraising goal of $20,000__ - **a dollar for each signature we submitted.**
---
| [**Can you pitch in $10, $25, $50 or even $100 to help us reach our goal and continue to grow our movement?**](https://GarrettForMichigan.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=db0473a6037c1be0f2423e4fd&id=c4ee0627f7&e=fe4b890356)

| | **Michigan's greatest asset is our people, Peter.** And it is a true honor to have so many phenomenal Michiganders at my side in this fight for our state's future.

_**Thank you for being a part of this campaign.**_

God Bless Michigan,

_Garrett Soldano_

body:

Friend -- This might be the shortest note you've ever received from me, but it's probably the one I'm most excited to send. Sofia and I are thrilled to introduce our son, first-born, and newest member of Country First (at least he was for a few minutes): **CHRISTIAN ADAM KINZINGER** Mom and Baby are doing great, and I'm over the moon to bear the most important title I'll ever hold: Dad. Country First,

Adam

name: Casey DeSantis
subject: Democrats' Disgraceful Attack
body:

name: Laura Loomer
subject: He’s dead last . . .
body:
Being a member of Congress can be a comfy job . . .
| | | [](https://libersend.loomered.com/l/bZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA/4YpRuJ9HMZi7wVAyP7BzCw/Hb47xoqXtceEuqV9b763r2892w) | | Please read the message below from our sponsor, Laura Loomer for Congress.
**We encourage you to[visit their site today](https://libersend.loomered.com/l/bZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA/4YpRuJ9HMZi7wVAyP7BzCw/Hb47xoqXtceEuqV9b763r2892w)**.
---

---
| [](https://libersend.loomered.com/l/bZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA/4YpRuJ9HMZi7wVAyP7BzCw/Hb47xoqXtceEuqV9b763r2892w)
Patriot,

[](https://libersend.loomered.com/l/bZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA/4YpRuJ9HMZi7wVAyP7BzCw/Hb47xoqXtceEuqV9b763r2892w) Being a member of Congress can be a comfy job . . .

. . . show up to work when you feel like it.

. . . work a little bit . . . maybe.

. . .rub elbows with the powerful Washington elites and have lobbyists fawn all over you for support.

But, you have to at least PRETEND to be serving your constituents by putting in a little effort to keep yourself from being DEAD LAST on Congressional rankings lists.

My RINO opponent, Do-Nothing Daniel Webster doesn't even bother to pretend.

**For four of the last five years, Webster has been DEAD LAST when ranked among his colleagues when it comes to the number of laws they individually enacted.**

And the one year he wasn't at the bottom of the list . . . **he was second to last!**

[**Help me replace Do-Nothing Dan by backing my campaign today.**](https://libersend.loomered.com/l/bZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA/4YpRuJ9HMZi7wVAyP7BzCw/Hb47xoqXtceEuqV9b763r2892w)

It's pathetic.

WE, the American taxpayers, are paying Webster $174,000 a year, not including his allowances, expenses, and Congressional benefits.

And what has he done?

**Nada! Zilch! Zero!**

Let's throw the Do-Nothing RINO's out of Congress and set an example starting with the removal of Dan Webster!

[**Chip in to help me beat him in the Republican Primary.**](https://libersend.loomered.com/l/bZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA/4YpRuJ9HMZi7wVAyP7BzCw/Hb47xoqXtceEuqV9b763r2892w)

We need to send a clear message to the Republican Establishment: Work for us, or be replaced!

[**So let 's send Do-Nothing Dan packing.**](https://libersend.loomered.com/l/bZPeCTAajmzyaMKwCIY0iA/4YpRuJ9HMZi7wVAyP7BzCw/Hb47xoqXtceEuqV9b763r2892w)

Thank you again for all that you do.

Respectfully,

Laura Loomer
